_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of web application security risks
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI application security risks, including prompt injection, jailbreaking, data exfiltration via LLM outputs, and model supply chain vulnerabilities.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Knowledge SP-K-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-012 (SP, cosine: 0.558), SP-K-011 (SP, cosine: 0.485).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.9/10 (raw 0.494). Shared vocabulary: [application, knowledge, risks, security]. NICE-only terms [web] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, chain, data, exfiltration, injection, jailbreaking] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.901 (P=0.946, R=0.860). P exceeds R by 0.086 — the NICE element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.0%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of web application security risks" and "Knowledge of AI application security risks, including prompt injection, jailbrea". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.575 > STS 0.494 (gap +0.081). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [application, knowledge, risks, security] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 23.8%. Shared tokens: [application, knowledge, risks, security]. NICE-only: [web]. WIDAI-only: [ai, chain, data, exfiltration, injection, jailbreaking].
